title: Prefer GitHub issues or PRs as canonical task descriptions (#1)
goal: Make WudiTask agent workflows use an existing or newly created GitHub Issue/PR as the canonical problem description whenever an owning repository is clear, with text-only descriptions reserved for cases without a suitable repository.
context: Canonical requirements: https://github.com/ChaoWao/wuditask/issues/1. Keep structured WudiTask goal/context/acceptance fields as a concise execution contract and store the canonical URL in links; do not add hidden remote mutations to the deterministic Python add command. This maintenance request originated while working in hw-native-sys/simpler at main@9c752a7d; leave that repository and its task state untouched.

acceptance_criteria.description: The operational WudiTask skill makes Issue/PR-first discovery or creation mandatory when an owning GitHub repository is clear, uses text-only description only when no suitable repository exists, stores the canonical URL in links, and applies the same rule to selfupdate fix without dropping required structured task fields.
Read-only CLI help for add, workflow, and selfupdate exposes the Issue/PR-first policy and remains non-mutating.

README, workflow, protocol/data-format guidance, and examples consistently distinguish the canonical Issue/PR narrative from the concise WudiTask execution contract; all tests and local data validation pass.

completion.result: Superseded after user clarification: direct WudiTask maintenance must not be represented as a WudiTask task or automatic GitHub Issue.

acceptance_results.evidence: Superseded after user clarification: direct WudiTask maintenance must not be represented as a WudiTask task or automatic GitHub Issue.
